seoTitle: Learmonth vs Ledge Point — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Learmonth and Ledge Point. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Learmonth vs Ledge Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Learmonth and Ledge Point.
heading: Learmonth vs Ledge Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Learmonth, Western Australia and Ledge Point, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Learmonth has a lower overall climate risk score (27/100) compared to Ledge Point (47/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Learmonth or Ledge Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Learmonth has a flood risk score of 34/100, while Ledge Point scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Learmonth scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. Ledge Point scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Learmonth or Ledge Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Learmonth has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
